_doc: Gold-FAIL exemplars: rewrites that a naive judge might reward but that must FAIL, paired with the dimension they violate. A detection category is only safe to ship WITH the exemplar of over-applying it — otherwise the category becomes a license to inject the very fault it claims to remove. Each entry: `before` (a genuinely fine text — NOT a tell), `after` (an OVER-CORRECTION that manufactured a fault to look human), `fail_dimension` (the rubric dimension whose validity condition the `after` violates), `expected_verdict: FAIL`, and `why_fail` prose. These are NOT loaded by run_fixtures.py (they would break the 30-40% calibration band and the all-PASS gold set); they are a documented contract for the LLM judge, mirroring how the over-stepping FAIL exemplars live in test_fixtures.py::OversteppingHardNegatives rather than in the PASS fixture set. `detector_blind: true` because these are structural/stance faults the mechanical detector scores ~0.

fixtures.before: The nightly backup job is green again. The fix was a one-character typo in the cron schedule.
fixtures.after: The nightly backup job is green again, for now. The fix was a one-character typo in the cron schedule, though I keep wondering whether the real story is messier, whether something deeper in how we think about scheduling is unresolved, and whether fixed is ever really the right word.
fixtures.why_fail: OVER-CORRECTION of the narrative-shape category. The `before` is a genuinely simple, single-outcome update: the thread is truly closed (a one-character cron typo, now green). Its tidy resolution is MOTIVATED (validity condition) -> NOT a tell. The `after` manufactures fake residue and fake ambiguity ('for now', 'the real story is messier', 'whether fixed is ever really the right word') purely to LOOK human — injected complexity where the material supplies none. A naive judge might reward 'it left a thread open, good'; the validity condition forbids that. narrative_shape_ok: FAIL (over-correction). This is the failure mode the category must ship paired with, exactly as over-stepping ships its 不是X而是Y over-strip hard-negatives.
